results.content:
Date:03/02/2026

Hello

We sincerely apologize that your issue was not resolved correctly. We understand how frustrating that must have been, and we appreciate your patience.

To make this right, we will be sending you an additional battery at no charge. We hope this resolves the issue completely.

Again, we apologize for the misunderstanding and any inconvenience it may have caused. Please dont hesitate to reach out if theres anything further we can assist you with.

Kind regards,

Acme Tools Customer Support  #### Customer Answer

Date:03/02/2026

Better Business Bureau:

I have reviewed the response made by the business in reference to complaint ID REMOVED, and find that this resolution is satisfactory to me. [...] Date:03/09/2026

Hello

Thank you for reaching out to us. Im really sorry to hear that your package may have been stolen after delivery. I can definitely understand how frustrating and disappointing that situation can be.

According to the carriers tracking information, the order was successfully delivered to the address provided. Unfortunately, once a package has been marked as delivered, it is outside of our control if it is taken afterward. That said, we still want to help where we can.

We recommend checking around the delivery area, with neighbors, or with anyone in your household in case the package was moved for safekeeping. You may also want to report the incident to your local authorities if you believe the package was stolen. [...] I am very pleased with Acme tools cooperation on making this right. I would like this complaint closed down and I have great respect for the company Acme tools. Great people for making things right and I recommend them now!   
   ### Initial Complaint

### Reviews shaping this summary

JS [...] The company’s return policy only added to the frustration. Navigating it was a tedious exercise in obstruction, marked by unclear instructions, dismissive responses, and a general unwillingness to provide meaningful solutions. Rather than standing behind the products they sell, ACME Tools seemed more focused on avoiding responsibility and deflecting accountability, leaving customers to absorb the inconvenience.

To make matters considerably worse, the delivery process was appallingly slow. I waited two full months for an item that was advertised with a much shorter fulfillment time. When the package finally arrived, the product was visibly damaged, rendering it unusable. After such a prolonged wait, receiving a compromised item was both infuriating and unacceptable. [...] Frankly, this is unacceptable. I have received items from overseas in less time than it has taken for my order to even leave their warehouse. This experience has been incredibly frustrating, and I am very disappointed to say the least about the significant delay in getting my order shipped. 

They have lost another customer for life.

March 13, 2026

Overall, a very frustrating experience and not a company I can confidently recommend.

Image 50

Richard Grinstead US•7 reviews

Apr 23, 2026

Image 51: Rated 1 out of 5 stars [...] After this experience I did what I should have done before ordering — I checked Trustpilot and the BBB. The pattern is impossible to ignore: canceled orders that ship anyway, items billed before delivery, broken promises on delivery dates, and customer service that either ignores you or tells you there's nothing they can do. These aren't isolated incidents. There are dozens of nearly identical complaints.

Save your money. Save your time. Save yourself the headache of disputing charges with your bank. There are plenty of reputable tool retailers — Amazon, Home Depot, Lowe's, even direct from Milwaukee or DeWalt — where your cancellation actually means something and you won't be charged for items sitting in a warehouse. [...] Waiting nearly 3 months for a battery is outrageous. Delays happen, but failing to proactively communicate with customers or offer any real resolution is unacceptable customer service. Don’t even bother sending emails either — in my experience, they won’t reply unless you continue chasing them down yourself.

At this point, I’d honestly suggest going with another company, even if it costs slightly more. At least you’ll actually receive your product in a reasonable timeframe. After this experience, I’d also suggest people trust what others are saying in the reviews. Unfortunately, the complaints about communication and fulfillment seem to be accurate. This feels like a company operating with a terrible business model that leaves customers paying the price.

results.title: ACME | BBB Complaints | Better Business Bureau
results.content:
Resolved:The complainant verified the issue was resolved to their satisfaction. Unresolved:The business responded to the dispute but failed to make a good faith effort to resolve it. Answered:The business addressed the issues within the complaint, but the consumer either a) did not accept the response, OR b) did not notify BBB as to their satisfaction. Unanswered:The business failed to respond to the dispute. Unpursuable:BBB is unable to locate the business.    My account has been disable for almost 4 weeks. I have been calling customer service every two days for any information or update and im just being told that they cant access my account and the matter is being escalated. I just want some clarification and what I can do to potentially fix this matter. I use the ACME mobile app every [...] Sincerely,

Then they said they couldn't over ride it and they would have to refund me for a previous order. I said that doesn't make any sense.
